title: US Negotiators Arrive in Pakistan for Ceasefire Talks with Iran
detail: US negotiators arrived in Pakistan on April 11 for ceasefire talks with Iran, continuing the Islamabad summit process as both sides seek a framework to keep diplomacy alive. The talks extend VP Vance's April 10 visit to Islamabad, with negotiators working to establish parameters for the first sustained US-Iran diplomatic channel since the war began on February 28. Pakistan has positioned itself as a neutral mediator, setting a modest goal: keep dialogue open rather than finalize any comprehensive deal.

title: Israeli Airstrike on Lebanese Government Building Kills Multiple Military Officers
detail: An Israeli airstrike on a Lebanese government building killed multiple Lebanese military officers on April 10, further straining the fragile US-Iran ceasefire framework amid the active Lebanon front. The strike on government infrastructure — rather than Hezbollah-specific targets — marks a significant escalation of Israel's Lebanon campaign. The killing of Lebanese Army officers threatens to widen the conflict and deepen international criticism of Israeli operations in Lebanon.

title: Hezbollah Rocket Attack Damages 1,500-Year-Old Church Inside Israel
detail: A Hezbollah rocket attack damaged a 1,500-year-old church inside Israel on April 10, marking continued cross-border exchange as the Lebanon front remains active alongside ceasefire diplomacy. The strike on a historic Christian religious site drew international attention, highlighting indiscriminate targeting amid the ongoing Lebanon front. The attack occurred as Hezbollah and Israel continued exchanging fire despite the US-Iran ceasefire framework nominally holding.

title: Israel Rejects Ceasefire with Hezbollah Ahead of Lebanon Talks
detail: Israel formally rejected a ceasefire with Hezbollah on April 11, 2026, ahead of Lebanon talks scheduled for next week. The rejection underscores Israel's intent to continue its Lebanon campaign independently of the US-Iran diplomatic framework. The move complicates broader ceasefire diplomacy as US negotiators are simultaneously pressing forward with Iran talks in Islamabad, while Israel insists the Lebanon front is a separate theater under Israeli operational control.
